text: Full Circle
34 ABY
Crestian Tarasar
Tatooine
Atsvara Tarasar (NPC), Crestian Tarasar, Kasha Tabasuri
Caspian Union, The Mission
Kasha reunites with Crestian on Tatooine, the world they first met.
The Mission - Mos Eisley: Tatooine

What had once been a long abandoned warehouse has been transformed in short order to serve refugees from the recent famine in the Arkanis Sector. Taken over by The Mission it now serves as the charity's base of operations in Mos Eisley. The large open space of the structure has been altered by Xi Charrian engineers into a four storey structure. It's form is largely utilitarian with little mind put towards the aesthetics.

The ground floor is compromised almost entirely of massive cafeteria for refugees and volunteers with a sizeable kitchen supporting it. The second storey serves as a hostel for the refugees and volunteers, it affords little privacy but the beds are reasonably comfortable. The third storey serves as a medical clinic, working to ensure the refugee crisis is not followed by the spread of any diseases. The top floor is devoted to office space for the workers who are facilitated The Mission's efforts throughout the sector.

%tFor the past several weeks, the place where the Presav of the Caspian Union could most easily be found was not any world of the Union, but instead found in a converted warehouse in Mos Eisley, donating some time each day to providing inoculations and other light medical care to stream of refugees from New Ator and Vasch who ended up here. Likewise in spite of Tatooine not even having a single Biscuit Baron, its twelve year old CEO could be easily found giving hands on oversight to The Mission's cafeteria.%r%tThe chaos the famine unleashed has largely been quelled. The millions of credits pouring into the sector have served to stabilise the situation, though the need for support was still significant.%r%tHaving finished his daily clinic for the day, Doctor Crestian can be found near the cafeteria conversing with Atsvara. The room full, though not overflowing, with refugees having their evening meal.

%tIt's been a long time since she's been back here on Tatooine. Far too long... and yet, there are some folks that still remember their 'Woodcarving Feline'. Sure, many of them are a lot taller than they were before, but still, they gather around her, watching her expectantly. And for what it's worth, the Horansi does not disappoint.%r%tAfter the crowd, which did consist of some new customers among the ones that came because they remembered all the love and care she had shown them, had dispersed, Kasha Tabasuri finally stands from her stall just outside the warehouse. She stands taller than she had when she first showed up here, by 0.15 meters to be exact. And as she smiles to old friends and to new ones as they depart, she sighs as she realizes just how much she's learned since that long year. And yet, how much has not changed.%r%tCollecting the remaining figurines and placing them into her satchel, where several more hand-carved statues rest, Kasha moves towards the cafeteria. All that talking and conversing makes a kitty hungry. And it has been a long day.. but a good day for her.

%tIt is Atsvara that spots Kasha first. Mashi Horansi did tend to stand out as unique in Mos Eisley, whether they grew in height or not. Then again Tatooine was full of lots of unique individuals, The golden-skinned girl waves over her friend and exclaims, "We have porf!" Atsvara's enthusiasm quickly attracted Crestian's attention, after all most of the excitement her daughter had shown of late tended to the performance on bits on for other people. Crestian turns around then to see Kasha too and smiles.

%tWell, to be fair, Kasha sticks out wherever she goes. Mashi Horansi aren't very common, even on her homeworld of Mutanda. Hearing the voice of a very dear friend, she smiles from ear to ear and waves back to Atsvera and makes her way over to her and Crestian. "Ahh, porrrrf," she purrs, her Mutandan accent still a prominent feature in her words, no matter how many years it's been that she's lived offworld, "I trrrruly love and apprrreciate yourr taste in food, Vara." As she looks to Crestian, she grins toothily at him, "How'd yourrr day go?" She eyes Atsvara, offering the same question to her as well.

%t"A little frustrating to be honest. Some of the people we see in the clinic didn't have access to the best medical care before. The downtrodden are like to not be able to deal with things like famines on the their own. So a lot of what I am doing the clinic is giving them access to vaccines they didn't have before....you know making sure it doesn't spread. But every so often is come across some youngling whose parents had access to better care for them and to didn't take advantage of it." Crestian takes a deep breath and adds, "It also look for a moment like we had someone with a case of Bledsoe's disease. Thankfully a false alarm, but it is pretty contagious."%r%tMeanwhile Atsvara rolls her eyes, perhaps finding something boring in her father's report on his day. "How can it be a frustrating day? We have porf. AND a new shipment of Crimson Nerf Energy drinks." She smirks at her father before turning back to Kasha, "How was /your/ day?"

%t"Porrrrf /does/ make all things more interrrresting," Kasha agrees with a laugh to the girl before looking back to Crestian, nodding her head solemnly, "But yes, I agrrree with you. It's a shame we can't do morrrrre for them on morrre of a longer terrrrrm." She looks back to Atsvara and she smiles, "Well, like yourr fatherrrr's report, mine is much the same. So many people werrrre so happy to see me and that was all well and good, but there was so many in need of our aid. I may not be able to offerrrr much in the way of medical assistance to them like your fatherrr has been able to.." She holds up her satchel, "Therrre's no better curre for what ails you than a new toy, I always say." She looks to Crestian and grins, "And don't you worrrry, I still have enough left overrrr for the auction. I also have some biggerrrr, more detailed statues I made just for the event."

%tAtsvara smiles, "I have something to add myself. I had my people on the look out for any vintage promotional items. Came across a very early Jolly Meal toy, pre-Clone Wars. Well technically it wasn't a Jolly Meal promotion....they had these 'Heroes of the Republic' commemorative holocubes. Naturally I myself have them all. But since we found this extra one of Jedi Master Ikrit, I am hoping we can nabs some credits for it. Or at least some good press."%r%tCrestian starts to head towards to food line remarking back, "You are both way ahead of me I want to donate something but I have been not been able to think of what just yet. I mean I have plenty of stuff, but what is going to be special and meaningful....while still be something I am willing to part with. I will probably be doing more buying than anything."

%t"Naturrrally," purrs Kasha, echoing Atsvara's word, grinning with a quiet laugh before nodding to her, "Oh, I surrre someone would bid on that. I'm just surrrrprised you were able to find another one. Though, I suppose being the CEO of the company helps out a lot." She winks to her as she follows behind Crestian, her tail swishing in the sand behind her, "Well, just donate some unused commemorative pins from your Presav campaign.... you did make some, rrrrright?" She hmms and looks around at those gathered, "What do you suppose the turrrnout will be for the auction, anyway?"

%t"An amusing thought, but I fear that sort of self endorsement would probably come across as tacky. But you know I probably should see what we have left from that campaign for future archiving. Been a real pain the arse trying to get ready for the next election when there is some much work to be done here." As Crestian reaches the point of the line where he can grab a tray, he adds, "I think the turnout should be decent. The great thing about a charity auction is that even people who don't care about what you are raising credits for may still come out to bid on the stuff being auctioned."%r%tAtsvara adds, "There will be a lot of side events to I think. Veruca and Zubindi are already on Corellia preparing to do a little fashion show the night before. That should raise some more credits. And convenient justification to take a little break from being here."

%t"Yeaaaaaah," Kasha says slowly as she too grabs a tray, "Auctions tend to brrrring out just about anyone and everrrryone that just wants to buy rrarre things. I just can't help but wonderrrr what my figurines and statues will go for. Even those that don't know me perrrrsonally, they would still be considered a part of Horrransi culturre and herrritiage so I have no doubt there'd be people interrrreested." The Mashi looks over to Atsvara, her purple eyes twinkling, "And that'll be fun to attend. They always do good worrrrk."

%tWhile the are other things on the menu, Crestian does goes for the porf. "Well I know I am interested. I can always use more art. After all I have a ridiculous number of offices to keep decorated." He looks to Atsvara, "You know you really need to catch up on office space. Have you decided yet where you are going to put the corporate headquarters?"%r%tAtsvara smirks, "Well maybe if you political stopped butting heads with each other so much. It wouldn't be such a loaded question of where to base ourselves. You should talk Tevas, how much time do you spend in your own capitol building?"

%tOf course, Kasha goes for the porf as well, as she always does every chance she can get to eat that delicious dish. As Crestian shows his interest, the tips of her ears turn a brighter shade of red, but hardly noticeable with her black fur. "Well, I'll tell you what.." she says softly, "If you get outbid on any of my work that you'rrre interrrested in, I can see if I have anything in my collection that you might like for yourrr offices." She looks to Vara and hmms, "What are your choices so far?"

%tUnsurprisingly Atsvara goes for the porf as well, "Well we have a short list of about ten places. Tagge Company is based on Tepasi so there has been some thought of moving operations back there in spite of having gone independent, would be a bit of nostalgic choice but not super useful. Largely ruled out Coruscant, it is big but hasn't been very relevant in years. Selene is a strong possibility as the Imperium capital, but that would not make the New Republic based franchisees happy....but of course we have had to rule out the New Republic given its lack of political stability and animosity towards the Imperium and Union."%r%tThe golden-skinned girl pauses for a moment not even waiting to sit down to take a bite of her porf, "Much of our Board of Directors, of course, have ties to the Union so Caspar or Kichnar Orbital Station are both viable choices, but not everyone is happy with the Union so we would really be tied to it there. Corellia would probably be the most neutral choice, on paper we kind of already based there. Before we went public with who we were and were just operating as the nebulous consultancy and investment firm Golden Bantha Group, we registered it on Corellia. Financial reporting regulations there are pretty lax and a few of us are friends with Prime Minister Blazer, so he provided good advice in flying under the radar."%r%tAs Atsvara heads towards the table that Crestian has staked out, she adds a couple more possibilities. "We are also considering Dreven and Sullust. Sullust has had a strong Biscuit Baron presence over the decades, and while it is an Imperium world it does not seem like one anyone would get offended at. Dreven was not initially on the list, it is really out of the way, but after spending time there Veruca Violet and I saw some possibilities. So much of its infrastructure is tied being an Imperial throne world, which it hasn't been for years. That really hurts the economy there. But means there are a lot of unused government buildings, and because so little is going on there we could really dominate."

%tKasha nods her head as her mind goes instinctively into mediation mode. You can take the Mashi out of the Council of Elders, but you can't take the Spiritual Advisor out of the Mashi. "Neutrrrality would be favorrable in this situation, I would have to agrrree with you. You don't want to take sides between the New Rrrrepublic or the Imperrrium." As she sits with Atsvara and Crestian, she hmms and scratches the fur on her chin, "Would Casparrrr or KOS be considerrrred neutrral enough? Or is the Caspian Union a strong enough faction in the galaxy to be considerrred a conflict of interrrrest?" She takes a bite of her porf and knots her brow, "I can definitely see why you'd hesitate on any of those options though... but I serrrrrriously doubt that any place is truly neutrral, rrrrright?"

%tCrestian quickly pipes up, "Well the Union has desired a better relationship with the Republic right along, but the fact we are close with the Imperium is something a lot of politicians in the New Republic can't look past. So sadly we would not be seen as neutral enough for some, but Biscuit Baron is going to appear like a Caspian company no matter what with the Presav's daughter as CEO, and other Caspian on the board. Though as far as being seen as a strong power, I think we are still underestimate by some. Though they are probably underestimating Atsvara even more."%r%tAtsvara suddenly has an epiphany, "That reminds me I still need to pop over to Mos Espa and go intimidate the local police for the hard time they gave me last year when I was getting Cricket released from custody. They clearly did not appreciate who they were dealing with."

%t"Touche..." Kasha says, her ears drooping a bit as she listens to Crestian and realizes that neutrality cannot happen, "...I mean... you could just double down then and just have the headquarters in Caspar then." She shoves more porf into her muzzle and shakes her head, "Bah tha's bahsi'..." She pauses a moment, raises a finger on her free hand and chews her food, swallowing it down before trying again, "But that's beside the point... and I'm sure you'll figure out in due time. Just know that if you need any help with any of your endeavors, just ask." When Atsvara has her eureka moment, she looks over to her again, fighting the urge to roll her eyes, "Remind me... What did Cricket do that time?" By this time, Kasha should have known quite a bit of that maniac robot and its crazy antics.

%t"Apparently he had tasered Axel Vichten's asshole or something like that. I am sure he must have deserved. Damn cops kept the taser. I mean can you imagine the dignity, the cops let you go but keep one of your body parts? Its obscene. I got ak to make him a better one to replace it." Atsvara looks very pleased and proud of herself, in between ravaging bites of porf.%r%tCrestian smirks and adds, "She left out the part where she some how got there to be a whole cathedral ceremony for the unveiling of the new taser, complete with invited friends and family that included a suspicious number of business executives and government officials. She even got Robot #7 to do a painting of Cricket as part of this all. 'The Apotheosis of Cricket' it is in cadracak's gallery in Xi Xin. I mean don't get me wrong Cricket is a friend of mine, but what Atsvara has done for his already massive ego...." His thought trails off into a bite of his porf.%r%tAtsvara looks no less pleased with herself, "I am just the product of a Sith education system. Can I help it if I am just better at empire building than other people? Befriend and building up the self-esteem of a deranged and wealthy Droids is just logical. But if you ever need to knock him down a peg, he apparently has an irrational fear of Xa Fel. Was not expecting that."

%tKasha just groans and rubs her temples with both hands. "Why am I not surprrrrised in the least?" Sometimes, she wonders why she even asks. Even so, it's all in good humor as she just laughs and shakes her head, "Most imprrressive... and just as crrrrazy as I had expected." And what more can she add to that bit of information? She just takes another bite of porf.

%tJust then, a young human child shyly approaches the table. "I-I w-w-as told you h-have..." she stammers, looking at Kasha and her satchel expectantly. Kasha smiles and pulls out a carved yeat figurine. But then, when Kasha wiggles the yeat's tail (which is movable), the figurine's legs move back and forth (thanks to a hidden cord inside the hollow toy that is attached to the legs and tail), causing the child to squee with delight, clapping her hands as the figurine is offered to her. Kasha smiles warmly as the child takes the toy and scampers off.

%tAtsvara takes great interest in Kasha's interaction with the younglings. She is at the point of her life that her own introduce in such toys is no longer personal. Professionally, however, she is in the business of marketing to younglings. "Too bad hand crafted toys like that can't go into a Jolly Meal. I mean I am sure we could have something similar mass produced but it just would not be the same."

%tKasha nods her head and looks back to Atsvara, her smile fading only a bit, "Yes, sadly, it loses its value and charrrrm when you make a couple of thousand of these in a cheaperrr fashion." She scratches her chin again, "I mean, if done in a limited run, would that work?" She eyes the child as she shows the toy to who Kasha assumes is her mother, her smile returning as she watches the interaction, "Handmade toys, in my opinion, feel so much more rrrrewarding forrr me. Seeing the look on their faces is worrrrth every minute I spent worrrrking on them."

%tThe golden-skinned girl pauses between savagely eating her porf and steeples her fingers in front of her face, "Well I mean there a promotions like are Secret Sidious one where there is a rare prize, so something like that could be done....but it would probably make more sense as a local promotion." The look of epiphany begins to appear again, "If we did open a restaurant on Mutanda or Justa....we could promote its opening with locally produced toys. Perhaps that might help allay concerns about the intrusion of kimach culture a bit."

%tTo this, Kasha nods her head quickly, "It would definitely help, that's for sure. Especially if the toys were made by Horansi. We are very cautious about offworld technologies, as you know." She doesn't need to preach to the choir about Mutunda and her people. If nothing else, she is positive that both Atsvara and Crestian know the ins and outs of the Horansi culture. "And I say it again, I would love nothing more than to have a Biscuit Baron on at the very least at the spaceport on Justa," she adds... good luck getting one on Mutanda, she knows though.

%t"Well it is food for thought," remarks Crestian. "Who knows maybe all the work you have been doing here Atsvara will help convince the right people to let you build one here on Tatooine one day too. This bit of Mos Eisley definitely needs some work. This building was actually abandoned before we showed up. Thankfully ak and his team were able to fix it up very quickly. Probably less intrusive than building someone new would have been. The aesthetic is terrible, but it be a little unseemingly if we spent too many credits making it look nice."%r%tThe Presav pauses for a bite of porf before adding, "You know it is funny I used to get kind of bored of Tatooine whenever I had to be here. I think now I have just gotten use to the place. Like don't get me wrong, I am very much looking forward to go to Corellia. Honestly I can do more helping fundraising there at this point than serving as volunteer here much longer. But this place is starting to feel comfortably familiar."

%t"I was stuck herrrrre for a yearr," Kasha reminds Crestian, wagging her forkful of porf at him, "Believe me, I know how you feel on both levels. You do get used to it, but it can still be verrry borrrring at times. Especially when all you see is sand most of the time." She looks down to her bare feet, "Not to mention that sand is so coarrrrse and rrrrrrrough... and it just gets everrrrywherrrrre..." Wait, didn't a certain Sith Lord say the same thing?? Nah, that'd be too weird. Sith aren't that petty.

%tAtsvara smirks, "You two are cute. Remember I have live MORE than half my life in space. Having a place where the world doesn't end at a bulkhead is a blessing. Even if it is hot and sandy. Even Mustafar was a fun place. Like all that lava, really koovy. Alas no Biscuit Baron there either, but there is the Silverwood location so still fun."%r%tCrestian sighs a bit, he is after all responsible for having raised his daughter that long in space. He did his best to make to bring the galaxy home to them, but it was an exile nevertheless, "Tatooine would still be better without the sand. Alas Johanna likes it the way it is. There is some evidence Tatooine was once a lush world, would love to see it get terraformed back to that. But Johanna would never hear of that."

%tKasha tilts her head back and forth as she weighs the two arguments, "Well, to be honest and fair, the sand has become the culture of the people of Tatooine. You take that away at this point and civilization here would struggle to adapt." She points in the distance, where she knows there is a moisture farm, "What would the moisture farmers do if they have lakes, rivers, and even oceans of water that can be easily harvested?"

%t"I know, I know. But still imagine what they could farm if they just had a river? There is the satisfaction that comes at achieving things that are hard. But how many of them are just barely scraping by. Whatever other aspirations they might have having to be forgotten because their lives are too hard and marginal as it is. Life is always going to find a way to add new struggles without having to be married to existing ones." Crestian takes a deep breath, "Though I suppose at this point if anyone of them really wanted to do something different....there are just so many ships taking refugees in and out at the moment that no one would have trouble leaving.
